member.office_address: 1032 Longworth House Office Building Washington DC 20515-3306

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Addison McDowell: Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations Programmatic Request Form
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Thank you for your interest in submitting an appropriations request to my congressional office for Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27). Below you will find a summary of the submission form for programmatic and language requests for FY27. Programmatic and language requests for FY2026 will not be considered.

The current deadline to submit programmatic and language to Congressman Addison McDowell's office is 12:00pm on Monday, March 9, 2026. This deadline is subject to change pending updates from the Appropriations Committee. You are encouraged to follow up with the office periodically for updates.

Please note that you must fill out the entire application. The Committee requires all information for submission, and failing to answer all the questions may jeopardize your request. Please provide us with the President's Budget Request level (if it is available at the time of submission) or previous year's PBR. If you are submitting multiple requests, you must provide an overall priority ranking for each request. Please fill out separate requests for language and programmatic funding. You must provide the agency, agency subdivision/office, and specific account to which your request applies or your submission will not be considered. If you have any questions regarding this form, please do not hesitate to contact the Congressman's office at (202) 225-3065 or email NC06.Approps@mail.house.gov.

sections.description: Information about the organization and point of contact submitting the request.

fields.label: Organization Completing Application
Requesting Organization Contact Information (Name, Affiliation, Email, Contact Number)
Requesting Organization Mailing Address
Requesting Organization Representative/D.C. Point of Contact (Must Include: Name, Affiliation, Email, Contact Number)

sections.description: Identify the subcommittee, agency, and account for the request.

fields.label: Please select the subcommittee of jurisdiction for this request:
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food and Drug Administration, and Related Agencies
Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
Energy and Water Development and Related Agencies
Labor, Health and Human Services, Education, and Related Agencies
Military Construction, Veterans Affairs, and Related Agencies
National Security, Department of State, and Related Programs
Transportation, Housing and Urban Development, and Related Agencies
fields.draft_value: Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies
fields.rationale: As specified in the reference values, NASA's Science Mission Directorate falls under the jurisdiction of the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Applicable Title/Agency within the bill (ex. "Department of Agriculture" or "Department of Transportation," etc.)
fields.rationale: NASA is the primary agency for this request, as specified in the reference values.

fields.label: Applicable bureau or office within the agency provided above (ex. "Indian Health Service" within HHS or "National Highway Traffic Safety Administration" within DOT, etc.)
fields.rationale: The Science Mission Directorate is the applicable bureau within NASA for this funding request.

fields.label: Applicable Program or Account (ex. "Indian Health Facilities" or "Highway Traffic Safety Grants," etc.)
fields.rationale: 'Science' is the formal appropriations account name for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Please identify the type of request.
fields.rationale: The request is for a specific funding amount ($9 billion) for an existing agency program (SMD), which is defined as a programmatic funding request.

sections.description: Provide current and historical funding information relevant to this request.

fields.label: Amount included specifically for this item in the President's budget request for FY 2027 (if not available, please include the FY26 funding level from the PBR):
fields.rationale: Per the SMD Topline Funding History, the FY2027 PBR is unknown, so the FY2026 PBR level of $3.908 billion is used as the backup requested by the label.

fields.label: Please provide the Fiscal Year 2024 funding level for this account:
fields.rationale: The FY2025 enacted level (representing the most recent stable historical figure provided in the history table) is used to satisfy the prompt's request for historical data. Note: The pre-filled value of $7.25B was ignored as it matches the FY26 Enacted figure, not FY24.

fields.label: Please provide the Fiscal Year 2025 funding level for this account:
fields.rationale: Based on the provided SMD Topline Funding History for FY2025 Enacted.

fields.label: Please provide the Fiscal Year 2026 funding level for this account:
fields.rationale: This is the FY2026 Enacted amount provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Type of funding request:
options: Support a funding increase for the account indicated
Support a funding decrease for the account indicated
fields.draft_value: Support a funding increase for the account indicated
fields.rationale: The requested $9 billion represents a significant increase over both the FY2026 enacted level ($7.25 billion) and the FY2025 enacted level ($7.334 billion).

fields.label: If requesting a funding increase or decrease, please provide the funding level you are requesting for Fiscal Year 2027:
fields.rationale: This is the specific funding ask for NASA's Science Mission Directorate provided in the context.

fields.label: If requesting a funding increase, please suggest an appropriate offset from this bill.
fields.rationale: As a constituent organization, we respectfully defer to the Subcommittee's expertise in balancing priorities within the CJS bill to identify appropriate offsets.

fields.label: Justification for supporting a funding increase, decrease, President's Budget Request, or elimination of the program:
fields.draft_value:
* This $9B request restores NASA Science to a growth trajectory following the disruption of the FY2026 budget cycle, representing a $1.75B increase over the FY2026 enacted level.
* Sustained investment is required to implement the scientific community's Decadal Survey priorities and ensure the U.S. remains the global leader in space exploration.
* China is rapidly expanding its space science ambitions with lunar and Martian missions; this funding ensures the U.S. continues to set the norms for 21st-century exploration.
* NASA Science maintains a unique industrial and innovation workforce that cannot be easily recovered if lost to funding instability.
fields.rationale: Justifies the $9B request ($1.75B increase) using strategic arguments about leadership, competition with China, and workforce stability.

sections.description: Provide details for bill or report language requests and district-specific impact.

fields.label: Please state the impact of this request on North Carolina's 6th Congressional District:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science has obligated over $270,000 across North Carolina's 6th District between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting high-tech jobs and academic research.
* A $299,616 grant to North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University in Greensboro supports critical research into bacterial adaptation for future lunar missions.
* Other key local recipients include the Research Triangle Institute ($279,464), UNC Greensboro ($210,801), and High Point University ($41,168).
* These investments strengthen the local STEM talent pipeline and support the regional aerospace and technology economy.
fields.rationale: Cites specific spending data for NC-6, including the NC A&T grant and other university/research institution totals provided in the fact sheet.

fields.label: Summary of Language Request:
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Exact Language Request:
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Related language from prior fiscal years:
fields.draft_value: - H.R. 6938, the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Appropriations Act of 2026, which enacted $7,250,000,000 for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Please explain why this request is needed and is a good use of taxpayer dollars:
fields.draft_value:
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the U.S. economy through industrial demand, innovation, and commercial spinoffs.
* NASA Science represents less than one-third of one percent of federal spending while delivering essential capabilities in weather forecasting and planetary defense.
* The program utilizes rigorously vetted Decadal Surveys from the National Academies, providing a nonpartisan basis for high-impact scientific investment.
* Sustaining this funding protects over 4,000 civil servant roles and thousands of contractor positions that form the backbone of the U.S. aerospace sector.
fields.rationale: Utilizes core advocacy talking points regarding economic ROI, the nonpartisan nature of Decadal Surveys, and workforce impact.

fields.label: Please state the impact of this request on North Carolina's 6th Congressional District:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science has obligated over $270,000 across North Carolina's 6th District between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting high-tech jobs and academic research.
* A $299,616 grant to North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University in Greensboro supports critical research into bacterial adaptation for future lunar missions.
* Other key local recipients include the Research Triangle Institute ($279,464), UNC Greensboro ($210,801), and High Point University ($41,168).
* These investments strengthen the local STEM talent pipeline and support the regional aerospace and technology economy.
fields.rationale: Repeated field; provides identical data-rich local impact response.

fields.label: Summary of Language Request:
fields.draft_value:
- No specific bill or report language is requested at this time.
- This request focuses on programmatic funding of $9 billion for the Science Mission Directorate.
- The goal is to provide funding stability and restore American leadership in space science following the disruptions in FY 2026.
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Exact Language Request:
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Related language from prior fiscal years:
fields.draft_value:
- House Report 118-466: Provides $7,334,200,000 for NASA Science programs in FY2025, emphasizing the importance of planetary science and astrophysics.
- H.R. 6938 (FY2026): Enacted funding of $7,250,000,000 for the Science Mission Directorate following proposed budget cuts.
- Title 51, U.S. Code: Codifies space science as a fundamental public responsibility for advancing U.S. security and economic competitiveness.
fields.rationale: The request type is programmatic; no specific bill or report language is being requested.

fields.label: Please explain why this request is needed and is a good use of taxpayer dollars:
fields.draft_value:
* Every $1 invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the U.S. economy through industrial demand, innovation, and commercial spinoffs.
* NASA Science represents less than one-third of one percent of federal spending while delivering essential capabilities in weather forecasting and planetary defense.
* The program utilizes rigorously vetted Decadal Surveys from the National Academies, providing a nonpartisan basis for high-impact scientific investment.
* Sustaining this funding protects over 4,000 civil servant roles and thousands of contractor positions that form the backbone of the U.S. aerospace sector.
fields.rationale: Repeated field; provides identical strategic and economic justification.

fields.label: Please state the impact of this request on North Carolina's 6th Congressional District:
fields.draft_value:
* NASA Science has obligated over $270,000 across North Carolina's 6th District between FY2023 and FY2025, supporting high-tech jobs and academic research.
* A $299,616 grant to North Carolina Agricultural and Technical State University in Greensboro supports critical research into bacterial adaptation for future lunar missions.
* Other key local recipients include the Research Triangle Institute ($279,464), UNC Greensboro ($210,801), and High Point University ($41,168).

sections.description: Information about priority and other submissions.

fields.label: Are you making this request to other Congressional offices? If yes, please list all offices receiving this request.
fields.draft_value:
* Reps.
* Don Bacon (R-NE) and Judy Chu (D-CA) are leading an existing multi-member House letter in support of this request.
* Request also being submitted to additional House offices.
fields.rationale: Request is being submitted to multiple offices; Bacon/Chu lead the House letter per reference values.

fields.label: Of the requests made by this group in this bill, this request ranks as priority [ ] of [ ] total requests:
fields.rationale: Priority ranking is specific to the submitter's total portfolio of requests and cannot be determined from the provided context.
